REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MATTHEW MUATH ZGHOUL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR CONSTRUCTIVE EVICTION AND BREACH OF CONTRACT FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE THAT HE VACATED THE PREMISES AND SPECIFIC EXPRESS TERMS OF THE CONTRACT THAT HAVE BEEN BREACHED. DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE OVERRULED. COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES LANDLORD-TENANT RELATIONSHIP (PARAGRAPHS 7, 2); NOTICE RE: REPAIRS; FAILURE TO REPAIR. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MATTHEW MUATH ZGHOUL'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT ISDENIED. COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS RE: CONSCIOUS DISREGARD FOR PLAINTIFF HEALTH AND SAFETY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Attendance At His Deposition And Imposing Monetary And Issues Sanctions Against Plaintiff For Non-Appearance And Unnecessary Delay
Off Calendar Jul-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances on FRI-JUL-15-2016:
Oksana Tsykova, Esq. of Law Office of Daniel Horowitz (T: 925.283.1863) for the Plaintiff / Opposing Party;
John Park Jennings, Esq. of Clapp Moroney Vucinich Beeman Scheley (T: 650.989.5400) for the Defendant / Moving Party.

DEFENDANT MATTHEW MUATH ZGHOUL'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Attendance At His Deposition And Imposing Monetary And Issues Sanctions Against Plaintiff For Non-Appearance And Unnecessary Delay is argued, GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).
The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's opposition to compel is untimely. Sanctions against Plaintiff's counsel under 473 is GRANTED in the amount of $900 payable to the Court.
Sanctions in the amount of $2,915 against plaintiff is stayed pending appearance by plaintiff at deposition.
Request for sanctions due to bankruptcy is DENIED.
CCP Section 128.5 sanctions against plaintiff is DENIED.
The Court orders plaintiff Sergei Korjavin-Uryson to appear on AUG-02-2016 at 10:00 AM in defendant's counsel's office. $2,915 sanctions are stayed pending plaintiff's compliance. Plaintiff's failure to appear may result in the Court lifting sanctions and adding additional sanctions.
Defendant's requests for sanctions against plaintiff's counsel for opposing continuance of trial date is DENIED.
Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: L. Fong; matter not reported. (501)

Off Calendar Jan-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT AJLOUN ENTERPRISES, LLC, IHSAN ZGHOUL, MOHAMMAD ZGHOUL, MUSAB ZGHOUL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. Plaintiff shall submit a proposed order for signature re: December 9, 2014 ruling on Matthew Zghoul's demurrer to complaint. Plaintiff shall re-submit First Amended Complaint for filing thereafter. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: R. Goulding; Dept.501; Not Reported

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Teela A. Crostwhite, Esq., (650-989-5400), for Matthew Zghoul, Defendant and moving party.
Daniel Aaron Horowitz, Esq., (925-283-1863), for Sergei Korjavin-Uryson, Plaintiff and opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MATTHEW MUATH ZGHOUL's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the causes of action for constructive eviction and breach of contract for Plaintiff to allege that he vacated the premises and specific express terms of the contract that have been breached. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Complaint sufficiently alleges landlord-tenant relationship (paragraphs 7, 2); notice re: repairs; failure to repair. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Teela A. Crostwhite, Esq., (650-989-5400), for Matthew Zghoul, Defendant and moving party.
Daniel Aaron Horowitz, Esq., (925-283-1863), for Sergei Korjavin-Uryson, Plaintiff and opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MATTHEW MUATH ZGHOUL's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is
DENIED. Complaint sufficiently alleges facts re: conscious disregard for Plaintiff health and safety. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
